title: Rhapidophyllum hystrix
taxonomy: Rhapidophyllum hystrix is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family. It is the only species in the genus Rhapidophyllum. This genus is part of the subfamily Coryphoideae. The plant is native to the southeastern United States. Rhapidophyllum hystrix is also known as the needle palm.

intro: Rhapidophyllum hystrix, also known as the prickly needle palm, is a small, evergreen palm tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an anti-diabetic agent.
